events.text: Patient is a 61-year-old Asian female of Korean descent who presented with progressive fatigue, unexplained weight loss of fifteen pounds, and recurrent infections over two months.
At age 61, the patient was diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia NOS, ICD-O morphology code 9861/3, primary site bone marrow C42.1, after evaluation for persistent leukocytosis with monocytosis.

At age 61, initial laboratory studies showed white blood cell count of 45,000/uL with 52% blasts and 12% promonocytes, hemoglobin 7.9 g/dL, and platelet count of 41,000/uL. LDH was 1,120 U/L. Coagulation studies showed fibrinogen of 178 mg/dL and D-dimer of 4.6 ug/mL.

At age 61, bone marrow biopsy with aspirate demonstrated a hypercellular marrow (95% cellularity) with 72% myeloblasts. Blasts showed monocytoid features with folded nuclei. Flow cytometry showed blasts positive for CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, CD14 (partial), CD64, HLA-DR, and MPO. The immunophenotype was consistent with AML with monocytic differentiation.

At age 61, comprehensive genomic profiling identified FLT3-ITD mutation with allelic ratio 0.58, DNMT3A R882H mutation, TET2 frameshift mutation, and SRSF2 P95H mutation. NPM1 was wild-type. TP53 was wild-type.

At age 61, cytogenetic analysis revealed a complex karyotype: 45,XX,del(5)(q13q33),del(7)(q22),-17,add(12)(p13),+21[14]/46,XX[6]. FISH confirmed deletion of 5q, monosomy 7, and loss of TP53 locus on chromosome 17. ELN 2022 risk was adverse based on FLT3-ITD without NPM1, complex karyotype, and monosomy 7.
At age 61, induction therapy was initiated with 7+3 cytarabine and daunorubicin plus midostaurin per the RATIFY protocol dosing schedule.

At age 61, during induction the patient developed disseminated intravascular coagulation with fibrinogen dropping to 62 mg/dL, D-dimer rising to 28 ug/mL, and prolonged PT to 22.4 seconds. She required cryoprecipitate and platelet transfusions with gradual improvement.
At age 61, neutropenic fever occurred on day eight with blood cultures growing Enterococcus faecium resistant to vancomycin, treated with daptomycin.
At age 61, day 14 bone marrow biopsy showed a moderately hypocellular marrow with 22% persistent blasts, indicating refractory disease.
At age 61, given primary refractory AML with adverse cytogenetics, the treatment plan was changed to FLAG-IDA salvage consisting of fludarabine, high-dose cytarabine, G-CSF, and idarubicin.

At age 61, FLAG-IDA salvage chemotherapy was administered with fludarabine 30 mg/m2 on days one through five, cytarabine 2 g/m2 on days one through five, G-CSF starting day zero, and idarubicin 8 mg/m2 on days three through five.

At age 61, the patient developed invasive pulmonary mucormycosis during aplasia following FLAG-IDA, with CT showing a reverse halo sign in the right lower lobe. Isavuconazonium was initiated and she required a prolonged hospitalization of 42 days.
At age 61, bone marrow biopsy following FLAG-IDA showed 8% residual blasts, failing to achieve complete remission. The patient was classified as having multiply refractory AML.
At age 61, given persistent disease and FLT3-ITD positivity, gilteritinib 120 mg orally daily was initiated as targeted salvage therapy.
At age 61, after four weeks of gilteritinib the patient achieved a composite complete remission with incomplete hematologic recovery (CRi) with 3.2% blasts and persistent thrombocytopenia.
At age 61, gilteritinib caused differentiation syndrome with fever, dyspnea, bilateral pleural effusions, and weight gain of eight pounds, managed with dexamethasone and temporary drug hold.

At age 62, the patient proceeded to allogeneic stem cell transplant from a haploidentical donor (daughter) with reduced-intensity conditioning given her comorbidities. Day plus 30 marrow showed 96% donor chimerism with 1.8% blasts.

At age 62, day plus 60 bone marrow demonstrated declining donor chimerism at 82% with rising blasts at 6%, consistent with early post-transplant relapse. The patient was transitioned to comfort-focused care given her wishes and multiply refractory disease course.
At age 62, CT chest abdomen and pelvis showed increasing splenomegaly to 19 cm and new small bilateral pleural effusions but no discrete extramedullary chloroma lesions.

documents.text:
CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, initial laboratory studies showed white blood cell count of 45,000/uL with 52% blasts and 12% promonocytes, hemoglobin 7.9 g/dL, and platelet count of 41,000/uL. LDH was 1,120 U/L. Coagulation studies showed fibrinogen of 178 mg/dL and D-dimer of 4.6 ug/mL.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 61, bone marrow biopsy with aspirate demonstrated a hypercellular marrow (95% cellularity) with 72% myeloblasts. Blasts showed monocytoid features with folded nuclei. Flow cytometry showed blasts positive for CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, CD14 (partial), CD64, HLA-DR, and MPO. The immunophenotype was consistent with AML with monocytic differentiation.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, cytogenetic analysis revealed a complex karyotype: 45,XX,del(5)(q13q33),del(7)(q22),-17,add(12)(p13),+21[14]/46,XX[6]. FISH confirmed deletion of 5q, monosomy 7, and loss of TP53 locus on chromosome 17. ELN 2022 risk was adverse based on FLT3-ITD without NPM1, complex karyotype, and monosomy 7.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 61, day 14 bone marrow biopsy showed a moderately hypocellular marrow with 22% persistent blasts, indicating refractory disease.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, given primary refractory AML with adverse cytogenetics, the treatment plan was changed to FLAG-IDA salvage consisting of fludarabine, high-dose cytarabine, G-CSF, and idarubicin.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 61, bone marrow biopsy following FLAG-IDA showed 8% residual blasts, failing to achieve complete remission. The patient was classified as having multiply refractory AML.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, after four weeks of gilteritinib the patient achieved a composite complete remission with incomplete hematologic recovery (CRi) with 3.2% blasts and persistent thrombocytopenia.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

At age 62, the patient proceeded to allogeneic stem cell transplant from a haploidentical donor (daughter) with reduced-intensity conditioning given her comorbidities. Day plus 30 marrow showed 96% donor chimerism with 1.8% blasts.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 62, day plus 60 bone marrow demonstrated declining donor chimerism at 82% with rising blasts at 6%, consistent with early post-transplant relapse. The patient was transitioned to comfort-focused care given her wishes and multiply refractory disease course.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 62, CT chest abdomen and pelvis showed increasing splenomegaly to 19 cm and new small bilateral pleural effusions but no discrete extramedullary chloroma lesions.

Please correlate clinically.

scenario_blurb: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with FLT3-ITD mutation. ELN intermediate/adverse risk. Induction with 7+3 plus midostaurin. Some achieving CR, some refractory. Include cytogenetics (normal karyotype or complex), blast percentage monitoring, allogeneic stem cell transplant in first remission for eligible patients.
